# Session Handoff
**Created:** 2026-03-16T03:50:54Z
**Branch:** master
**Project:** paperclip
**Trigger:** Session end (auto-generated)

## What Was Happening
**Two criticals:**
1. **ToolSearch triple-fire at close-out (34th consecutive occurrence).** L68 + L70, 5601ms apart, both post memory-agent-spawn. The HARD STOP rule has not resolved this in 34 sessions. Rule-based fixes are exhausted — only explicit context retention of the audit tool name at session start will break this.
2. **Memory ordering violation — worst recorded (+57038ms).** Mark-done (L64) fired 57038ms before memory agent spawn (L67). Root cause: L66 handoff-archive bash (mkdir+mv) inserted between mark-done and memory spawn. Correct sequence: spawn memory → mark done → archive handoff.
**One positive worth noting:** Zero violations in the main Optimizer session (L1-L70) for the first time. The 2 bash_ls violations in the JSONL belong to the harness-audit agent itself (both fired post-L71). The CLAUDE.md Optimizer Glob rule is working.
**New finding:** The harness-audit agent contaminates the session JSONL with its own violations. Future audits should exclude tool calls after the `Skill:harness-audit` invocation timestamp when computing the subject session's metrics.
Outputs written:
- `/Users/aialchemy/.claude/audits/harness-audit-904606b2-2026-03-15.json`
- `/Users/aialchemy/.claude/audits/2026-03-15-optimizer-ais40-highimpact-skill-builder.md`
- `/Users/aialchemy/.claude/audits/learnings.md` updated (ToolSearch count → 35, memory ordering regressed, session type variance improved, new Harness-Audit JSONL Contamination pattern added)</result>
Full transcript available at: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-aialchemy-projects-business-paperclip/904606b2-e6d2-452f-a8e0-eddd3df8cbad/tasks/a87c37f2a3bc6f3ee.output

## What Was Being Done
Memory saved. Two entries captured — optimization results and the first-time skill setup gotcha.
Score: **0.69**. Two criticals noted:
1. **ToolSearch triple-fire** — 34th consecutive. The HARD STOP rule isn't sticking. Real fix is retaining the audit tool name from session start, not relying on rules alone.
2. **Memory ordering** — worst ever (+57038ms). Wrong sequence: mark-done → archive → memory-spawn. Right sequence: memory-spawn → mark-done → archive. The `mkdir+mv` I inserted between mark-done and memory spawn is what blew it.
One genuine win: **zero main-session violations** for the first time across all Optimizer runs. The Glob rule for file discovery is holding.
Interesting new finding from the auditor: the harness-audit agent itself pollutes the session JSONL with its own `bash_ls` violations — future audits should cut off at the `Skill:harness-audit` invocation timestamp.
